subject: Update aaf templates to deploy service
commit_message:
Update aaf templates to deploy service

Add help charts for aaf compoenets to the oom.
Adjust nodeports according to the wiki nodeport
list.

Issue-ID: AAF-114
Change-Id: Ib324cdd7c068ccb9d2fa4056fd155c9b6ac21e0c
Signed-off-by: Sai Gandham <sg481n@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

(7 comments)

There are some missing licenses, extra spaces and duplication of identities.dat that can be improved. There are more places than I commented. Otherwise it looks OK.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1 Verified-1

NodePort 95 (30295) is already in use by clamp.
Please make sure all other ports are not in use by other components as well.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

We are trying to use new charts and we are not able to run new images, I tried to run aaf_cm manually and found below errors (I had to create the log file manually otherwise default image fails by saying log file does not exist):

root@dev1-aaf-cm-5658c7d6fc-mrx5s:/# mkdir -p  /opt/app/osaaf/logs/cm/
root@dev1-aaf-cm-5658c7d6fc-mrx5s:/# /opt/app/aaf/cm/bin/cm >> /opt/app/osaaf/logs/cm/stdout`date -I` 2>> /opt/app/osaaf/logs/cm/stderr`date -I`

        at org.onap.aaf.auth.server.Log4JLogIt.<init>(Log4JLogIt.java:79)

Patch Set 2:

Ports are modified according to Nodelist wiki and spaces remvoed

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1 Verified-1

Unfortunately, I think the node port wiki is out of date.

You have many, many conflicts that need to be addressed in some way before this can be merged.

./aaf/charts/aaf_cm/values.yaml:  nodePort: 98
./uui/values.yaml:  nodePort: 98

./vnfsdk/values.yaml:  nodePort: 97
./aaf/charts/aaf_fs/values.yaml:  nodePort: 97

./clamp/values.yaml:  nodePort: 95
./aaf/charts/aaf_hello/values.yaml:  nodePort: 95

./aaf/charts/aaf_locate/values.yaml:  nodePort: 94
./multicloud/charts/multicloud-windriver/values.yaml:  nodePort: 94

./aaf/charts/aaf_oauth/values.yaml:  nodePort: 93
./multicloud/charts/multicloud-ocata/values.yaml:  nodePort: 93

./aaf/values.yaml:  nodePort: 99
./uui/charts/uui-server/values.yaml:  nodePort: 99

The best way to move forward is to ask the question do each of these containers need to be accessed by a human client (GUI, REST, etc..)?  If so, only then do you need a node port.  If the answer is no (pods/containers running in the kubernetes cluster need to reach these services) then we can work with you to drop node ports from your chart.

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

Hi All,

I am working on these charts and will raise a review which will include all the changes posted by AAF team alongwith improvements required to run AAF using the charts.

Thx,
Mahendra
